news.title: Over 880 illegal colonies drain sewage into Hindon, Uttar Pradesh tells NGT
news.para: The UP government informed the National Green Tribunal that 888 unauthorized colonies are discharging 40 MLD of sewage daily into the Hindon River. Additionally, 51 open storm drains in the basin are carrying an estimated 1,067 MLD of mixed pollutants into the river and its tributaries.

news.title: Millenia WB Yield Fund commits ₹41 crore for villa project in Hyderabad
news.para: WhiteBoard Advisory and Management, along with Casagrand, have secured SEBI approval for a Rs 500 crore fund. This capital will fuel growth for residential projects, with initial focus on Chennai, Bangalore, and Hyderabad. The fund aims to deliver yield-based returns to investors. Casagrand, a leading developer, is also pursuing an IPO.

news.title: Micro Mitti launches information technology park on 2.3 acres land in Indore
news.para: Micro Mitti has launched its IT park, Cybercity, in Indore, Madhya Pradesh, spanning 2.30 acres with 6.28 lakh sq ft of built-up area. The project will offer 77 office and 13 retail units, focusing on leasing with a total leasable area of 4.48 lakh sq ft.  Investment stands at ₹300 crore, with completion anticipated by 2029.

news.title: Court upholds Nagpur civic body's eviction drive on prime Subhash Road land
news.para: A district court has upheld the Nagpur Municipal Corporation's eviction of illegal occupants from a prime 15,000 sq m land parcel. The lease, granted in 1937 to Abhyankar Smarak Trust, expired in 1967 and was never renewed.

news.title: Dwarka projects to earn Delhi development body ₹29,000 crore in licence fees
news.para: Delhi Development Authority is shifting to long-term land leasing, expecting over Rs 29,700 crore from three Dwarka projects over 55 years. A five-star hotel, a Transit Oriented Development, and a super-speciality hospital will generate substantial annual licence fees, boosting infrastructure and DDA's revenue streams.

news.title: UP-RERA enables online complaints against excess transfer charges by promoters
news.para: The Uttar Pradesh Real Estate Regulatory Authority has made online complaint filing operational for allottees facing demands for transfer charges beyond the prescribed limits. Complaints can be filed through Form-M on the UP-RERA portal under the e-court mechanism, following the implementation of the 10th amendment to its General Regulations, 2019.

news.title: Maharashtra to introduce property cards with valuation for all plots under proposed Land Titling Act
news.para: Maharashtra is set to introduce a Land Titling Act making property cards mandatory for all land parcels, reflecting their value. This move aims to provide globally valid proof of ownership, simplify transactions, and reduce property disputes. The legislation is expected to be finalized by July and presented to the assembly during the monsoon session.

news.title: SRA scraps 181 of 228 government joint venture projects in Mumbai
news.para: Nearly two years after initiating joint ventures for slum redevelopment, 181 out of 228 projects have been scrapped due to lack of progress and feasibility issues. State-run agencies like BMC, Mhada, and MMRDA will now implement fewer projects, with the remaining returning to SRA for redevelopment under new schemes.

news.title: Punjab minister Sanjeev Arora sent to seven-day ED custody
news.para: Punjab Industries Minister Sanjeev Arora faces a week in Enforcement Directorate custody. He is implicated in a Rs 100-crore GST fraud and money laundering case. The court deemed the allegations serious, requiring further investigation into the money trail. Arora's lawyer claims the case is fabricated. The Enforcement Directorate searched his company, Hampton Sky Realty Ltd.

news.title: Goa's town planning department to lock 82 lakh sq meters under no-development zones
news.para: Goa's TCP department is designating over 82.8 lakh sqm of land as non-developable, marking a significant move to protect ecologically sensitive areas. This initiative, driven by forest department recommendations, aims to preserve natural cover, sand dunes, orchards, and water bodies across Salcete, Mormugao, Sattari, and Pernem for future generations.

news.title: PMRDA likely to hand over Mahalunge-Maan town planning scheme to PMC for implementation
news.para: The stalled Mahalunge-Maan Town Planning scheme is finally progressing. The Pune Metropolitan Region Development Authority will transfer the project's execution to the Pune Municipal Corporation. This move follows the merger of villages into PMC limits. The scheme aims to develop a 250-hectare area near Hinjewadi IT park. It promises improved infrastructure and planned urban growth for the region.

news.title: Gurugram: Builder dupes client of over ₹15 crore, held
news.para: Gurugram Police arrested a 50-year-old real estate firm owner for allegedly duping individuals of crores. The accused, Mahesh Mugam, promised land agreements for 11.33 acres in Palwal district and received Rs 17 crore. He failed to register the land and subsequently cut off contact with the complainant.

news.title: Hubballi-Dharwad civic body extends 5% property tax rebate till May-end
news.para: The Hubballi-Dharwad Municipal Corporation has extended its 5% property tax rebate until the end of May due to lower-than-expected collections. The drive began late, prompting the extension to boost the Rs 235 crore target for 2026-27, with Rs 90 crore collected so far. GIS work will also be offered free in July and August.

news.title: Maharashtra govt to move SC against Bombay HC order on 254 acre Mira-Bhayander land: Revenue minister
news.para: Maharashtra is preparing to take its fight to the Supreme Court after a Bombay High Court ruling favored two builders' claims to 254.88 acres in Mira-Bhayander. With the land's worth in crores, the government maintains it holds rightful ownership. Revenue Minister Chandrashekhar Bawankule declared that they will vigorously defend their position, aiming to keep this critical asset in government hands.

news.title: Fresh 39A proposals seek conversion of over 25,000 sq meters in Bardez, Pernem
news.para: The Town and Country Planning department has proposed converting over 25,000 sqm of land across Goa, including natural cover and orchard areas, to settlement zones. Nine proposals, cleared by the TCP board, are now open for public objections. Major conversions include a 18,733 sqm plot in Bardez for KGL Realtors and 9,750 sqm in Pernem for IRAP Infra LLP.

news.title: Uncertainty over acquisition of over 500 acres land for New Nagpur
news.para: The New Nagpur Project faces a major hurdle due to escalating land acquisition costs. Originally estimated at Rs3,000 crore, the expense has now surpassed Rs4,000 crore. This surge may force authorities to reduce the project's land acquisition by nearly 500 to 600 acres. Farmers are urged to complete formalities to avoid project delays and potential loss of benefits.

news.title: Pune: 12 metro stations on Hinjewadi-Shivajinagar route likely to open by June-end
news.para: Pune's Hinjewadi-Shivajinagar Metro Line 3 is poised for a partial opening by June-end, with 12 stations from Maan to Balewadi expected to become operational.  Officials confirmed the project is 95.2% complete, awaiting safety clearances.  Full corridor operations, including Shivajinagar, are anticipated by October, pending resolution of technical delays.

news.title: Luxury tax on large houses in Kerala garners ₹157 crore in five years
news.para: Luxury tax on residential buildings generated Rs 157.62 crore for the state exchequer over five years, with FY2025-26 alone contributing Rs 67.66 crore. Increased revenue is attributed to a growing number of large houses and intensified tax identification efforts. The tax applies to houses exceeding a prescribed area limit, with exemptions for smaller homes and rebates for advance payments.
